ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICTION FOR TRO AND OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-12-24 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, TRO AND OSC, RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S OCTOBER 30, 2024 ORDER AND THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE AS OF NOVEMBER 8, 2024. HOWEVER, SINCE DEFENDANT FILED AN OPPOSITION ON THE MERITS, THE REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONSIDERED AND IS DENIED ON THE MERITS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, IN PART AND SUSTAINED, IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, TO BE PLED AS A REMEDY AND NOT AS A STANDALONE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE REMEDY OF SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE, THE INADEQUACY OF A LEGAL REMEDY, AND SPECIFICALLY WHAT TERM IN THE AGREEMENT IT SEEKS ENFORCED, IN LIGHT THAT THE PROPERTY AT ISSUE IS ALREADY SOLD. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER ON THE GROUND OF STANDING IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO ADDRESS 11 U.S. CODE 349(B)(1)(3) ON REPLY. DEFENDANTS' CITED AUTHORITY ONLY ADDRESS CASES WHERE A BANKRUPTCY CASE HAS BEEN CLOSED, RATHER THAN DISMISSED. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE IS PREMISED ON DEFENDANTS' VIOLATION OF THE AGREEMENT TO ACCEPT MONTHLY PAYMENT AND NOT FORECLOSE. (FAC 24-25.) WITH RESPECT TO TENDER, DEFENDANTS' CITED AUTHORITY SUGGESTS TENDER IS REQUIRED WHEN THERE IS AN ALLEGED IRREGULARITY IN THE NOTICE OR PROCEDURE OF THE SALE, NOT WHEN THERE IS A CHALLENGE TO THE TRUSTEE'S RIGHT TO CONDUCT THE SALE AT ALL. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: HARRY L. JACOBS ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Defendant's Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, in part and SUSTAINED, in part with leave to amend.

The First Cause of Action for Specific Performance is sustained with leave to amend, to be pled as a remedy and not as a standalone cause of action. Plaintiff must allege facts giving rise to the remedy of specific performance, the inadequacy of a legal remedy, and specifically what term in the agreement it seeks enforced, in light that the property at issue is already sold.

Defendants' demurrer on the ground of standing is overruled. Defendants failed to address 11 U.S. Code 349(b)(1)(3) on reply. Defendants' cited authority only address cases where a bankruptcy case has been closed, rather than dismissed.
Defendants' demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Wrongful Foreclosure is overruled. Plaintiff's claim for wrongful foreclosure is premised on Defendants' violation of the agreement to accept monthly payment and not foreclose. (FAC 24-25.) With respect to tender, defendants' cited authority suggests tender is required when there is an alleged irregularity in the notice or procedure of the sale, not when there is a challenge to the trustee's right to conduct the sale at all.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Harry L. Jacobs ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

TRO AND OSC, RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Nov-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

TRO AND OSC, RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet its burden for preliminary injunction. The Court Notes that the moving party failed to comply with the Court's October 30, 2024 order and there is no proof of service on file as of November 8, 2024. However, since Defendant filed an opposition on the merits, the request for preliminary injunction is considered and is denied on the merits.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
